input: {"questions":[{"question":"Your working tree has 278 dirty entries (2 modified files + 276 untracked, mostly new `.claude/commands/*.md`). `/update-loa` needs a clean tree. How should I proceed?","header":"Dirty tree","options":[{"label":"Commit current state first (Recommended)","description":"Create a commit on feat/awareness-layer-spine with the current changes, then run /update-loa. Safest — nothing is lost and the merge is auditable."},{"label":"Stash and pop after merge","description":"git stash -u → run /update-loa → git stash pop. Faster but riskier per .claude/rules/stash-safety.md (pre-commit hooks can shift stash slots)."},{"label":"Abort — let me clean up manually","description":"Stop here. You'll handle the working tree yourself and re-invoke /update-loa later."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"The Loa upstream remote is named `loa-upstream`, not `loa`. How should I treat it?","header":"Remote name","options":[{"label":"Use loa-upstream as-is (Recommended)","description":"Just fetch from `loa-upstream main` for this update. Non-destructive, no config change."},{"label":"Add a `loa` alias remote","description":"Run `git remote add loa <same-url>` so future /update-loa runs match the skill's expected name."},{"label":"Rename loa-upstream → loa","description":"Run `git remote rename loa-upstream loa`. Clean but changes existing remote config."}],"multiSelect":false}]}
